description_long: This is a roughly circular, shallow bowl with a visibly uneven rim and a matte, dark brown to gray surface. The bowl appears handmade, with irregularities and a slightly warped shape, suggesting it may be crafted from clay or a similar earthen material. Several chips and cracks are visible along the rim and body, with one prominent section missing, exposing the thickness of the material. The surface is unglazed and has a rough, almost stone-like texture, with some lighter patches and discoloration. There are faint, worn markings or inscriptions on the interior, possibly indicating provenance or maker information. The overall appearance is utilitarian and aged, with clear signs of wear and damage.
description: A shallow, handmade clay bowl with a rough, matte surface, visible chips, and cracks. The bowl is dark brown-gray with signs of age and wear.
description_view_0: The bowl is seen from a slightly elevated angle, showing a large chip on the right rim and faint interior markings.
description_view_1: This view highlights the largest missing section on the rim and more pronounced interior inscriptions.
description_view_2: The bowl's back is visible, showing a relatively intact rim with some discoloration and surface roughness.
description_view_3: A frontal view of the bowl's most damaged side, with a large crack and missing rim section clearly visible.
attribute_location: kitchen or outdoor communal eating area
attribute_context_of_use: serving or holding food, possibly for communal or utilitarian use
